Division of Globe XIV 176
Sindhu-Sauvira is identified with Sindh-Sagar Doab, the region
between the Jhelumand the Indus. In the Junagadh inscription also
the two parts appear as one geographical entity. According to the
Ksmasutra Sindhude^a lay to the west of the Indus.
Alberuni states that Sauvira denotes Multan and Jahravar.
In Sanskrit the word Sauvira is used in the sense of 'sour gruel',
'jujube fruit', and 'antimony'. It is also used in combination as in
Sauvimftjana. It is possible that in ancient times great heroes and
warriors hailed from this region. Hence the name, inarta is men-
tioned in the Girnar inscription. According to the Mahabhsrata
(II. 14-60) Kusasthall or Dvaraka was situated in Anarta.
Phenagiri is placed near the mouth of the Indus by Monier-
Williams.
Karnapmveya is synonymous with Karna prnvaranarazntiontd in
the Ramayana (IV. 40.26) according to Kern.
Puraiava is the son of a Brahmana by a ^tidra woman. Here
